Fig. 6. Comparison of predicted moments up to third order across all regimes. ∥⟨u⟩∥2 denotes the Euclidean norm of the mean state of u, and Mijk = E[uiujuk] denotes third-order mixed moments; for example, M122 = E[u1u2 2], M133 = E[u1u2 3], M233 = E[u2u2 3], and M123 = E[u1u2u3]. The orange curve (FEX-TFDM) corresponds to the two-stage framework using FEX combined with TFDM. The pink curve (FEX-SRAN) represents FEX combined with SRAN, while the green curve (FEX-VAE) denotes FEX combined with VAE-based activation.
Fig. 7. Comparison of predicted fourth- and fifth-order moments across all regimes. M(k) denotes moments of

Fig. 8. Comparison of predicted covariance structures across three regimes: equipartition, forward cascade, and dual cascade. Orange (FEX-TFDM, time-independent) and blue (FEX-TFDM, time-dependent) denote FEX-based symbolic models coupled with time-independent and time-dependent TFDM activation, respectively.

Fig. 5. Coefficient performance of the nonlinear interaction terms across three different regimes under different training samples, evaluated using the same operator sequence within each regime. In dual cascade regime, red curve overlaps with the blue curve performance.

Fig. 2. Binary-tree representation in FEX. Each node in T corresponds to a unary or binary operator. A depth-1 tree ( L = 1), consists of a single operator, while deeper trees ( L > 1) compute outputs recursively through hierarchical composition of operators.

Fig. 4. Coefficient performance of the nonlinear interaction terms across three different regimes under varying noise levels, evaluated using the same operator sequence within each regime. Here, Bi denotes the coefficient of the nonlinear term, and B̂i denotes its predicted value.
Table 1 Recovered deterministic vector fields across turbulence regimes by FEX and Weak SINDy. Coefficients are rounded to three decimals and negligible terms are omitted for readability.
